key_insight: HiMuon replaces full-matrix Muon’s global Newton-Schulz update with independent tiled Newton-Schulz updates, reducing work by localizing spectral interactions to each tile.
engineering_relevance: This makes Muon-style optimization more practical for large dense layers by enabling smaller GPU kernels, batching, chunking, and flexible tile-size schedules with lower compute cost.
method: Partition each momentum-gradient matrix into T×T tiles.
Apply the same finite Newton-Schulz map independently to every tile.
Reassemble tiled updates, preserving interactions within tiles while discarding cross-tile coupling.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports transformer-training experiments and controlled matrix-function diagnostics showing improved optimizer-step efficiency while staying close to full-matrix Muon in the tested regimes.
watch_item: For finite tile sizes, HiMuon is a local matrix-function map rather than a convergent approximation to the full-matrix update, so behavior can depend on tile boundaries and schedule choices.
paper_title: Hierarchical Muon: Tiled Newton-Schulz Updates for Efficient Muon Optimization
